channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Anil Ambani files defamation suit against NDTV over reports linked to Adani takeover
item.description: Businessman Anil Ambani approached the Delhi High Court alleging that NDTV had published several defamatory reports targeting him to assist the Adani Group’s alleged attempts to acquire his companies....

item.title: Authorities Monitor Hantavirus Cases on MV Hondius Cruise Ship
item.description: The MV Hondius, a Dutch cruise ship, is currently under close monitoring after a hantavirus outbreak affected several passengers. The World Health Organization confirmed seven infections and three dea...

item.title: Kenyan Teen Becomes Registered Nurse in US After Four Years of Hard Work
item.description: A Kenyan girl, Liet, moved to the United States at 19 with her mother, Judy Nyakerario Misoro, carrying nothing but a suitcase and big dreams. Faced with the common immigrant dilemma of earning quick ...

item.title: Saskatchewan Hockey Programs Produce Top WHL Talent in 2026 Draft
item.description: The 2026 WHL Prospects Draft highlighted the depth and quality of Saskatchewan’s hockey development programs. The Regina Pats made a headline-grabbing move by selecting goaltender Fletcher MacDonald n...

item.title: Virgin Australia launches Velocity Platinum Plus loyalty tier for high-spending frequent flyers
item.description: Virgin Australia has introduced a new loyalty tier called Velocity Platinum Plus, aimed at its most frequent flyers. This elite level is designed to offer a distinct mark of recognition, including a V...

item.title: SGPC Chief Revises Stand on Punjab Anti-Sacrilege Law, Raises Concerns Over Religious Interference
item.description: The president of the Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee (SGPC), Harjinder Singh Dhami, has reversed his earlier support for Punjab’s recently enacted anti-sacrilege legislation, now calling it a ...

item.title: Alto Restores Public Comments After Accidental Removal During High-Speed Rail Consultation
item.description: Several Canadians who submitted feedback on Alto's proposed high-speed rail project were initially alarmed when their comments were removed from the project's interactive map. Jackson Garrett, an 18-y...

item.title: David Licauco Confirms He Is Fine After Minor Injury at Bida sa Arena
item.description: During the recent Bida sa Arena event, Filipino actor and athlete David Licauco experienced a minor injury that caused concern among his fans and attendees. Despite the scare, Licauco reassured everyo...

item.title: Mahua Moitra files complaint after facing harassment on IndiGo flight
item.description: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra recently reported an incident of harassment during her travel on IndiGo flight 6E 719 to Delhi for a parliamentary committee meeting. She alleged that a group of men...

item.title: US and Iran exchange fire again, threatening fragile ceasefire
item.description: On May 7, 2026, hostilities between the United States and Iran resumed, raising concerns over the stability of the ceasefire agreed on April 7. Iran's top joint military command accused the US of brea...

item.title: Tensions Escalate Between U.S. and Iran Amid Strait of Hormuz Incidents
item.description: On May 7, 2026, tensions between the United States and Iran sharply escalated after military exchanges in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. military conducted strikes on Iranian military targets followin...

item.title: Kodak Black Arrested in Florida on Drug Trafficking Charges
item.description: According to recent reports, rapper Kodak Black was arrested in Florida on charges related to drug trafficking. Law enforcement authorities reportedly took him into custody as part of an ongoing inves...

item.title: Government Implements Measures to Prevent Antiquities Smuggling
item.description: The Central Government has initiated a stringent mechanism to prevent smuggling of antiquities and curb illegal trade in priceless art treasures. The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has taken ste...

item.title: Shakira and Burna Boy Announce Official 2026 World Cup Song 'Dai Dai'
item.description: Colombian superstar Shakira don join forces with Nigerian music giant Burna Boy to drop the official song for the 2026 World Cup, wey dem title 'Dai Dai'. The teaser video wey dem release dey show Sha...

item.title: Congress observes emerging frontrunner for Kerala leadership post
item.description: The article discusses the latest developments in the political scenario of Kerala, where the Congress party is observing the emergence of a new frontrunner for a key leadership post. The competition f...

item.title: BINI receives official invitation to perform at Japan's Summer Sonic Festival, shares Lauren Dyogi
item.description: Lauren Dyogi, a prominent figure in the Philippine entertainment industry, recently shared insights on how the P-pop girl group BINI received an official invitation to perform at Japan's prestigious S...

item.title: PTI Senators Protest Lack of Transparency Over Bushra Bibi’s Hospital Transfer
item.description: On Thursday, PTI senators staged a walkout from the Senate after their parliamentary leader, Syed Ali Zafar, raised serious concerns regarding the overnight transfer of Bushra Bibi from Adiala jail to...

item.title: WA records eighth consecutive budget surplus amid strong mining royalties
item.description: Western Australia has recorded another substantial budget surplus, marking the eighth consecutive year of positive financial results for the state Labor government. Since taking office in 2017, the go...

item.title: Kisii Student Forced to Work in Brickmaking Due to Unpaid School Fees
item.description: In Kisii County, a Grade 10 student named Victor Orenge has been forced to turn to brick making after being sent home from school due to unpaid fees. Victor joined the school in the first term, but wi...

item.title: Celebrating Sir David Attenborough's Century of Nature Broadcasting
item.description: Sir David Attenborough, born on 8 May 1926, marks a remarkable 100 years of life and an extraordinary career bringing the wonders of the natural world into homes across the globe. From a childhood spe...
